summary: Frontend-only regression testing completed for latest AI suggestion and UI updates. Verified active-paragraph suggestion behavior, X-dismiss behavior, suppression-until-new-typing behavior, reappearance after typing in same block, and editor/header/toolbar/menu rendering with no regressions.
action_items: No blocking fixes required for requested scope.
Keep monitoring AI suggestion timing UX since inline suggestion generation is MOCKED locally.
critical_code_review_comments: Suppression logic is correctly keyed by blockId+content (suppressedSuggestionMap[block.id] === block.content), which prevents immediate reappearance after dismiss until new typing occurs.
handleBlockChange clears suppression for the edited block before recomputing AI, enabling suggestion return only after new input.
Inline AI suggestion generation and collaboration choreography remain MOCKED (frontend-local), so behavior does not validate live LLM/network reliability.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated against public URL from frontend/.env (https://realtime-editor.stage-preview.emergentagent.com). Screenshots: /app/test_reports/iteration4_editor_loaded.jpeg and /app/test_reports/iteration4_post_checks.jpeg. Verified: suggestion appears for active paragraph, dismiss via X hides it, does not return without typing, returns after new typing in same block, and toolbar/menu still render.
rca of the issue: No blocking issue reproduced in requested scope. Behavior works because dismiss stores exact current content as suppression baseline; new typing changes content and intentionally invalidates suppression.
